skill.name: use-conductor
skill.description: Scan conductor/ directory for project direction, workflows, and task planning when present
skill.summary: Scan conductor/ directory for project direction, workflows, and task planning when present
skill.author: 89jobrien

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and task markers in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, shell commands, cryptographic operations, network scanning, or prompt injection attempts were found. The remaining risk is low because the skill asks the assistant to read and update local conductor planning files.

low_findings.title: Local Conductor File Access
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to check for a conductor/ directory, read planning files, and update plan.md status markers. This is expected behavior, but it can read local project documentation and modify task-tracking files if the user allows edits.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains direct instructions to read conductor files and update plan status markers. The behavior is local and bounded to project planning files, so the severity is low.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown
low_findings.description: The external command, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The flagged locations are Markdown inline code, a directory tree, and task status markers, with no executable shell, Ruby, cryptographic algorithm, or network scanning instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the flagged text is Markdown documentation, not executable code. No file contains shell execution syntax, cryptographic operations, or reconnaissance behavior.

content.value_statement: Project planning files can be missed when starting work in a repository. This skill directs Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use conductor files for project context and task order.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a conductor/ directory exists at the project root.
Reads product, guideline, technology, workflow, and track files when present.
Uses tracks.md and active track plans to identify current work.
Interprets conductor task markers for not started, in progress, and completed work.
Directs the assistant to follow documented project workflow and scope.
limitations: It depends on a repository having a conductor/ directory.
It does not create a conductor project structure by itself.
It does not validate JSON or Markdown file correctness.
It can only follow plans that are accurate and current.

use_cases.title: Start Work From an Existing Plan
use_cases.description: Find the active track and next task before changing code.

use_cases.title: Maintain Repository Workflow Discipline
use_cases.description: Use documented workflow, scope, and style guidance while completing tasks.

use_cases.title: Review Project Direction Quickly
use_cases.description: Summarize product goals, standards, and current tracks from conductor files.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Next Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Check for a conductor/ directory. If it exists, read the relevant files and tell me what task I should work on next.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Project Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the conductor files to summarize the product direction, coding standards, workflow rules, and active tracks.

prompt_templates.title: Work Within an Active Track
prompt_templates.prompt: Use conductor/tracks.md to find the active track. Read its plan and spec, then help complete the next open task.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Plan Alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the current repository state with the conductor workflow and active plan. Identify mismatches, stale tasks, and next actions.

output_examples.input: What should I work on next?
output_examples.output: The assistant checks conductor files, identifies the active track, and reports the next open task with relevant scope notes.

output_examples.input: Summarize this project before I contribute.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a concise overview of product purpose, standards, technology choices, workflow, and active work.

output_examples.input: Help me finish the current conductor task.
output_examples.output: The assistant follows the active plan, updates task status when appropriate, and keeps work within the documented specification.

best_practices: Ask the assistant to read conductor files before starting repository work.
Keep tracks.md and active plan files current.
Review proposed task status changes before accepting file edits.
anti_patterns: Using the skill when no conductor directory exists.
Treating stale conductor plans as authoritative without review.
Allowing task status updates without confirming completed work.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It tells the assistant to inspect conductor project files and use them for context, workflow, and task planning.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No evidence found of command execution. The skill is Markdown guidance for reading and updating planning files.

faq.question: Does it require a specific conductor structure?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects files such as product.md, workflow.md, tracks.md, and track plans under conductor/.

faq.question: Can it change files?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to update task status markers when work starts or finishes, if edits are allowed.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Developers, leads, and contributors who manage project work through conductor planning files.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low local filesystem risk and dismissed the static high-risk alerts as Markdown false positives.
